scenes.title: A Rumour of Embers
scenes.intro: A half-frozen wanderer is carried in from the passes, and before the hearth-rune he weeps — he knows this fire. "Yours is not the only one," he rasps. "When the Tower fell we broke the Flame and ran, each with a shard, to hearths no enemy could find. I have seen another still burning — north and east, in a hold whose name I dare not say aloud."

advisors.text: If he speaks true, Archon, then Runehold is not alone — and a Flame gathered back to itself is a Flame that might one day be tended whole again.
Or it is bait. Every fire out there has enemies, and following a stranger's map into the north is how covens end.

choices.label: Mark the road — the other Embers must be found

win.text: You set the wanderer's words to memory and to map. It is only a rumour, but the coven has a purpose now beyond survival: somewhere the Flame still burns in pieces, and the pieces can be sought.

choices.label: Tend your own fire; the north can keep its ghosts

win.text: You give the wanderer broth and a corner by the hearth, and let his rumour cool. Runehold has trouble enough of its own.

scenes.title: A Strayed Herd
scenes.intro: A herd of Elysian ponies — manes like banked coals, eyes too knowing — drifts down from the high passes and lingers in Runehold's lower meadow. They are no one's, for now.

advisors.text: Meat and hides through Deepfrost, Archon. The meadow will not feed us; they might.
Ride them! Tame one and you have a friend out of the Elysian Fields. Eat one and you have a sad winter dinner.

choices.label: Take the herd for meat and hide

win.text: The meadow runs red and the granary fills. The coven eats; something old in the high passes takes note.

choices.label: Try to gentle and keep the herd

win.text: Patient hands and quiet runes win them over. Runehold has mounts now, and a little wonder it had forgotten.

lose.text: The herd will not be held. By morning they are gone over the ridge, and you are left with trampled fields.

scenes.title: A Warrior at the Gate
scenes.intro: A Warrior war-band makes camp at the edge of Runehold's fields. Their captain — sword on her back, a hawk on her glove — sends word: she will guard your borders against the things the Fracture spits out. For a price, or for a pact.

advisors.text: Steel we cannot cast, but steel does not forget how to kill. Make the pact. Pride is cheap when the Imps come.
They cannot touch the Flame, and so they fear what we are. Bind ourselves to them and the coven will mutter.

choices.label: Swear a pact of mutual defense

win.text: You clasp the captain's forearm. Warriors walk your walls that night, and for the first time in a season the watch sleeps.

choices.label: Pay them in coin, owe them nothing

win.text: Coin changes hands; the war-band patrols a season and asks no loyalty. A clean bargain, while it lasts.

choices.label: Turn them away — Wizards keep their own walls

win.text: You thank the captain and decline. The coven approves; the dark beyond the fields does not.

scenes.title: The Envoy from the Gilded Reach
scenes.intro: A Red Wizard in counting-house silks arrives from the Gilded Reach — smiling, with a ledger under one arm and an offer on his lips.

advisors.text: The Red cult never smiles for free. But coin buys grain, and grain is no metaphor.

choices.label: Pay tribute to secure Red favour

win.text: The ledger closes with a soft clap. You are, for now, a friend of the Stock Sanctuary.

choices.label: Refuse — Runehold is no one's debtor

win.text: The envoy's smile thins as he goes. Word will travel.

scenes.title: Overtures to the Red
scenes.intro: A Red factor you once snubbed sends a careful letter. The Stock Sanctuary, it seems, would rather profit from Runehold than feud with it — if you will make the first gesture.

advisors.text: Enemies cost more than tribute. Swallow the pride, Archon. Red gold spends the same as any.

choices.label: Send a generous tribute to mend the breach

win.text: The gift is received with a counting-house smile. The feud cools to mere commerce.

choices.label: Offer Red factors trade rights in Runehold

win.text: Red stalls rise in your market square. Coin trickles in, and so does Red influence you cannot quite see.

choices.label: Let the feud stand

win.text: You burn the letter. The coven likes a leader who holds a grudge — until the price of one comes due.

scenes.title: The Athenaeum's Price
scenes.intro: A grey-robed reader of the Forgotten Athenaeum comes north with a locked book and a proposal: Blue will share what Runehold has forgotten — for a tithe of coin, and a debt of deference.

advisors.text: I trained under their lamps. The Athenaeum's word is good and their books are better. Pay them.
Deference to Blue today is deference to Blue forever. Memory we can earn ourselves — slower, but ours.

choices.label: Pay the tithe and take the book

win.text: The reader unlocks the book and lets your scribes copy through the night. Runehold remembers a little more of itself.

choices.label: Offer a working of your own in exchange

win.text: You trade craft for craft. The reader leaves impressed, and Runehold keeps its coin and its pride.

lose.text: Your working falters under the reader's polite gaze. The book goes home unopened, and word of the fumble travels.

choices.label: Send the reader away — Runehold bows to no library

win.text: The reader bows, unbowed, and goes. The coven stands a little prouder over an emptier shelf.

scenes.title: The Cold Hearth
scenes.intro: The hold the wanderer named is a ruin, and its hearth is grey ash gone hard as stone. You are too late by a lifetime: this Ember guttered out long ago, and the little hold that kept it went out with it. Only char-marks on the walls remember there was ever a fire here — that, and a single scorched book the cold spared.

advisors.text: Read the book before the wind takes it. A hearth that died has more to teach us than one that lived — this is how our own fire ends, if we let it.
The Athenaeum would give a great deal for what's on these pages. And so, Archon, should we.

choices.label: Take the scorched book and study the dead hearth

win.text: The pages tell of the slow going-out: workings that took twice the mana, then ten times, then none at all — until the last keeper could not remember the word for fire. A grim gift, but a gift. You understand the Forgetting a little better now, and how near it always is.

lose.text: The book crumbles to flakes before you can read it deeply. You carry away only the cold, and the knowledge that another Ember is gone for good.

choices.label: Wake an ember from the cold ash with your own Flame

win.text: You touch a shaving of your own hearth-rune to the dead ash — and for one impossible breath, it glows. It does not last. But you have proven the Flame remembers itself across its shards, and that is worth more than the mana it cost.

lose.text: The cold ash drinks your Flame and gives nothing back. Worse — something in the dead hearth stirs at the offering, and does not settle when you leave.

scenes.title: The Coven Divided
scenes.intro: The Circle quarrels openly now. Two camps have formed at the hearth, and both look to you to name which Runehold will be: a coven of careful memory, or a coven that dares the Flame.

advisors.text: Tell them we are keepers of memory first. Caution has kept us alive this long.
Tell them we are children of the Flame. A coven that fears its own god deserves no apprentices.

choices.label: Declare for memory and caution

win.text: You name Runehold a house of remembrance. The careful are heartened; the bold go quiet, and a little cold.

choices.label: Declare for the Flame and daring

win.text: You name Runehold a house of the Flame. The devout blaze up; the cautious bite their tongues and watch.

choices.label: Refuse to choose — hold the Circle together

win.text: You speak for an hour and somehow both camps leave believing they won. It will not hold forever. It holds tonight.

lose.text: Your careful words satisfy no one. Both camps decide you are weak, and say so.

scenes.title: The Disputed Relic
scenes.intro: Your diggers unearth a pre-Burning reliquary — a coil of rune-etched copper that hums when touched. Word travels fast. Within a week both Red and Blue send envoys, each certain the relic is theirs by right.

advisors.text: Red will pay in coin we can spend this winter. Sentiment does not fill a granary.
Sell it to a counting-house and it vanishes into a vault. Give it to the Athenaeum and it is studied, copied, remembered.

choices.label: Sell the relic to the Gilded Reach

win.text: Red coin floods in; the copper coil disappears into a Stock Sanctuary vault, and Blue does not forget the slight.

choices.label: Entrust the relic to the Athenaeum

win.text: Blue scholars weep over the coil and share their findings freely. Red marks the lost sale in a ledger somewhere.

choices.label: Keep the relic and study it yourselves

win.text: Your own scribes coax the coil's secret loose. Both cults leave empty-handed and faintly insulted.

lose.text: The coil stays silent for you, and now both cults know you hoarded it for nothing.

scenes.title: The Drowned Conjuror
scenes.intro: Apprentices sorting the salvage bring you a water-swollen tome, giggling and uneasy. It is the account of Maelor, who in the last bright age tried to raise a drowned city whole from the seabed — towers, gardens, ten thousand souls — in a single working. The final pages are written in a shaking hand: he could summon the towers, then only the gardens, then only a single drowned bell, and at the last could not recall the word for 'up'.

advisors.text: He did not fail because he was weak, Archon. He failed because the memory was going out from under him mid-working. Read it as a warning: the further we reach, the further we have to fall.
A city from the sea! Give me the mana and I'd finish what Maelor started. He simply ran out of Flame — we haven't yet.

choices.label: Set the apprentices to copy and study the tome

win.text: You have the tome copied twice against loss, and its lesson read aloud in the hall: great workings die not from too little power but from too little memory. The coven is wiser, and a little more afraid.

choices.label: Shelve it — the coven needs hope, not omens

win.text: You lock Maelor's drowned city away where the apprentices won't brood on it. Some mornings a keeper needs to believe the reaching still works.

scenes.title: The Fading Rune
scenes.intro: A master-rune carved into the hall's keystone has begun to blur. Left alone, its working will be gone by the next thaw.

advisors.text: Memory is the only wealth the cataclysm cannot burn. Pour mana into the archive while we still can.

choices.label: Pour mana into the archive

win.text: Apprentices chant through the night; the rune deepens and holds.

choices.label: Let it fade — we have more pressing needs

win.text: By morning the keystone is smooth and silent, and no one alive recalls what it said.

scenes.title: The Flickering Door
scenes.intro: A door breathes light at the foot of the stair. Behind it, the Sacred Flame waits — and the coven is forgetting faster than it can learn.

advisors.text: We forget faster than we learn. The Flame remembers everything. Use it.
And what walks back out wearing our faces? I have buried Souls before, Archon.

choices.label: Send an apprentice into the Flame

win.text: She returns wreathed in old knowledge, eyes bright and strange. The lost runes are lost no longer.

lose.text: What returns is not her. The Circle does not speak for three days.

choices.label: Seal the door. Some things should stay forgotten.

win.text: You wall the stair with rune and stone. The light goes out beneath your hand.

scenes.title: The Hungry Season
scenes.intro: The stores run thin before the Highsun harvest. The coven looks to you with hollow eyes.

advisors.text: The Warriors of the eastern road carry full wagons. We are Wizards — let us not starve like sheep.
Raiding the Warrior's Guild is how feuds begin. Buy grain. We can afford pride or we can afford winter, not both.

choices.label: Raid a Warrior caravan for grain

win.text: The raid succeeds; wagons roll into Runehold heavy with grain.

lose.text: The Warriors were ready for you. You return with wounded and little else.

choices.label: Trade coin for grain

win.text: A fair price, fairly paid. The stores fill, and no blood is owed.

scenes.title: The Last True Name
scenes.intro: Piece by piece, the failure-tales have begun to rhyme. In every one, a great Wizard reaches, and the memory goes out beneath them at the worst moment. Tonight your eldest reader closes the last book with a shaking hand and says what none of you have dared: "It is not that we grow weak. The world is forgetting how magic is done — and it began when the Flame was broken. The Flame was never only holy, Archon. It was memory. It was how the world remembered magic at all."

advisors.text: Then every Ember is a shard of the world's memory, and Runehold holds a piece of the only thing that can hold the Forgetting back. Gather them, and we do not merely survive — we remember.
If that's true, the ones who broke the Flame doomed us all to forget, to keep it from an enemy. I would like to know who that enemy was — and whether it is still hunting the shards.

choices.label: Set it down in plain words: the Flame is the world's memory

win.text: You write the truth into the coven's book at last, and the whole shape of your saga changes. Runehold is not merely a hold to keep alive — it is a keeper of memory, and the Embers are the road back from the long forgetting.

scenes.title: The Rival Hearth
scenes.intro: This hearth still burns. A hold no larger than Runehold keeps its Ember behind a stone door and a wary Circle of its own, and its Archon meets you on the threshold with a working half-shaped in her hand. "We know what you carry," she says. "We know what you want. Two Embers are stronger than one — but not if one hold swallows the other."

advisors.text: Two shards tended together burn nearer to whole. Treat with them, Archon. This is how the Flame comes home — not by conquest.
Or take theirs. One great fire, in one strong hold, is worth more than two frightened ones. They would do the same to us, given the nerve.

choices.label: Swear an accord between the two hearths

win.text: You clasp hands over both hearth-runes, and for a moment the two Embers lean toward each other like they remember being one Flame. An accord is sworn: not a gathering yet, but the first thread of one.

lose.text: The words come out too grasping, and the other Archon's door closes in your face. You have not made an enemy — but you have not made a friend, and the road home is long.

choices.label: Take their Ember by force of magic

win.text: It is quick, and it is ugly. You leave with two Embers where you came with one — and with the certain knowledge that every other hearth will now bar its door to Runehold, and sharpen something for you.

lose.text: Their Circle was ready, and yours was not. You are driven off with nothing, and word will run ahead of you: Runehold reaches for other holds' fires. That name will cost you.

scenes.title: The Sealed Stair
scenes.intro: Something scratches behind the sealed door, patient and unhurried. The mortar is cold to the touch, and getting colder.

advisors.text: It remembers that we shut it out. Walls have a way of becoming doors again.

choices.label: Reinforce the seal with deeper runes

win.text: You bind the stair with deeper wards. The scratching stops — for now.

choices.label: Press your ear to the stone and listen

win.text: For a single heartbeat, a voice you knew. Then nothing. The Flame's Regard stirs in your chest.

scenes.title: The Tower That Forgot
scenes.intro: A trader from the Bastion swaps you a Story for a sack of grain: the tale of the White wizard Serevane, who bound a fortress to walk on stone legs and carry her people out of the Fracture's path. It walked for a year and a day. Then, one still morning, it forgot how a wall holds up a roof — and came down all at once, with everyone she had meant to save still inside.

advisors.text: Order is the first thing the Forgetting takes, Archon. Serevane's fortress did not break — it lost the memory of being whole. As may we all.
One story, told for grain. It may be true; it may be a Bastion's bad dream dressed as history. Weigh it — don't kneel to it.

choices.label: Record the Story in the coven's own book

win.text: Serevane's walking fortress joins your slow-growing library of the ways magic has failed. A grim shelf — and the truest map you own of the road the whole world is walking.

choices.label: Trade the Story onward for coin

win.text: A grim tale sells as well as a glad one. You pass Serevane's fall along the market roads and pocket the difference.

lose.text: No one wants to buy an omen this season. The Story stays with you, heavier for having tried to sell it.

scenes.title: The Trembling Ground
scenes.intro: At Deepfrost the ground shudders and a seam of cold light splits the hillside above Runehold — the Fracture, surfacing. Through the crack, something the colour of nothing turns over in its sleep.

advisors.text: The old wards can close it, but the working will cost us. Better cost than catastrophe.
Or we go down and look. Whatever sleeps there knows things the Flame itself forgot.

choices.label: Pour mana and lore into sealing the seam

win.text: Apprentices chant until their voices fail; the seam knits shut and the light goes out. The hill is whole, and so are you — barely.

choices.label: Descend into the seam to learn what sleeps there

win.text: You return wide-eyed with a fragment of the world's first memory — and the seam, satisfied, closes behind you.

lose.text: What you find down there finds you back. You climb out changed, and the seam stays open, breathing.

scenes.title: The Wanderer in the Ash
scenes.intro: A figure walks out of the ash-haze at dusk and stops at the edge of the firelight. A Forgotten Soul — robes you almost recognize, a face the Flame has rewritten. It does not speak. It waits to be known.

advisors.text: It came to us. The Flame sends nothing by accident. Let it in.
I have seen what wears a familiar face and means us harm. Keep the gate shut, Archon.

choices.label: Welcome the Soul to the fire

win.text: It sits among you and, in a voice like settling embers, gives up a rune the living had lost. By dawn it is gone, but the knowledge stays.

lose.text: Whatever sat at your fire that night left something wrong behind it. The coven sleeps badly for a long while.

choices.label: Turn the Soul away into the dark

win.text: You bar the gate. It stands a long moment, then walks back into the ash. The coven breathes easier; you do not.

scenes.title: Ash on the Wind
scenes.intro: Grey ash drifts in from the east for three days running. The old ones say the Fracture is breathing again.

choices.label: Record it in the saga and move on

win.text: You note the omen. The coven mutters and watches the horizon.

scenes.title: The Long Dark
scenes.intro: The sun comes up wrong three mornings running — dim, and a finger's width south of where it should be. Across the Runiverse, holds light their hearths early and speak in low voices.

choices.label: Walk among the coven and steady them

win.text: You make the rounds, hearth to hearth, until the muttering settles into resolve.

choices.label: Record the omen and let it pass

win.text: You set it down in the saga. The dark does not lift, but it does not deepen.

circle.name: Ember
circle.bio: Young, reckless, and drawn to the Sacred Flame like a moth to its namesake. The Circle worries about her. She does not.

circle.name: Korr Ashen
circle.bio: Survived the Great Burning with the scars to prove it. Has buried more Souls than apprentices, and will tell you so unprompted.

circle.name: Vela
circle.bio: Keeper of Runehold's half-burned Athenaeum-scrolls. Believes memory is the only wealth the cataclysm cannot take, and argues it at every Circle.

circle.name: Wisp
circle.bio: Quiet and devout, already half-listening to something the others cannot hear. Speaks of the Flame as though it has spoken to him first.

codex.title: Introduction
codex.blurb: The world you inherit, and the fire you keep.

entries.body: Once the Sacred Flame was tended whole in the Secret Tower, and the world was whole with it. Then its keepers vanished, the Tower went dark — and the world began, slowly, to forget. Magic thins, runes slip from memory, and the great powers of the old age have dwindled to a scatter of guttering hearths in the dark.

You are the appointed Archon of Runehold — though who named you, and why, is itself half-forgotten. As the Tower's enemies gathered, the coven that kept it broke the Flame into Embers and scattered them beyond reach. One was carried to your hearth-rune: the coven's heart, and its last thread to the old power. Let it die, and Runehold dies with it.

Beyond your walls the seven Colour Cults circle — each hoarding what magic remains, and no two of them allies for long. Win one to your side and you make an enemy of another. There is no pleasing all seven.

Your Wizards will counsel you, and quarrel. Famine, feud, and worse are coming. Lead them through it — and, if the old stories are true, seek the other Embers before the dark finds them first. Keep the flame, or be forgotten.

codex.title: The Pressures
codex.blurb: Seven meters and your standing with the Cults. Every scene reads and writes them.

entries.body: Your spellcasting reserve. Powers workings, quests, and defenses. Regenerates slowly, spent in bursts.
Food for the coven in a broken world. Famine is a constant low threat; a coven cannot eat runes.
The mercantile axis, ruled by the Gilded Reach and the Red Cult. Buys what magic and farming cannot — and ties you to aristocrat politics.
Runes and memory: the signature meter. Unlocks great workings, but DECAYS every season — 'The Forgetting' made mechanical. Maintain libraries, train apprentices, or watch power slip away.
The coven's morale — whether your Wizards believe you can lead them through the dark. Driven by Circle relationships and consistency with your values.

The cosmic meter. Drawing on the Sacred Flame is the highest reward and the highest risk: salvation, transformation, or undesirable loss. VEILED by default — you feel its pull through what your choices cost, but the running number is hidden. Reveal it under Options → Display.

The doom track. The cataclysm is not finished — Imps, stirring Beasts, and the slow Forgetting advance it. Reaches its limit and Runehold is consumed. VEILED by default — read its mood on the Map (quiet → stirring → rising → dire) rather than a number; reveal the exact meter under Options → Display.

codex.title: The Seven Color Cults
codex.blurb: Seven independent reputations. Acting for one often costs another.

entries.body: the Gilded Reach — greedy aristocrats and the Stock Sanctuary. The mercantile and coin power of the Runiverse.
The Bastion — the largest libraries and the Forgotten Athenaeum. The lore and knowledge power.
The chroniclers have not yet fixed this cult's character.
The chroniclers have not yet fixed this cult's character.
The chroniclers have not yet fixed this cult's character.
The chroniclers have not yet fixed this cult's character.
The chroniclers have not yet fixed this cult's character.

codex.title: Powers & Perils
codex.blurb: The forces your saga is written against.

entries.body: Once tended whole in the Secret Tower, the greatest power and the greatest loss in the world. When the Tower's keepers vanished and its enemies gathered, the coven that kept it broke the Flame into EMBERS and scattered them to hidden hearths rather than let it be seized. Draw on an Ember and you may work great magic — but Wizards who step too far into it become Forgotten Souls (roughly one in ten, something undesirable).

The shards of the Sacred Flame, carried out of the Secret Tower and scattered so no enemy could take the whole. Each is the burning heart of a hearth like Runehold. How many survived, and where, is unknown — kept, lost, gone cold, or in the wrong hands. Reuniting the Embers is the long road back to re-lighting the Tower. You hold one; there are others to find.

The world is literally forgetting itself — magic thins, runes slip from memory, and once-simple workings unravel. In game terms Lore decays every season unless held. Tomes and Stories the coven recovers point to a single cause: the Forgetting began when the Flame was broken. What that truly means, the wise are only starting to grasp.
The cataclysm that tore the world apart is not finished. It is the long game beneath all the others — the doom that takes Runehold if neglected.

Your advisor ring is rolled fresh each campaign — Wizards, each with a school (colour), a class, a rank, an age, four competence stats (Power, Wisdom, Guile, Courage) and a temperament (Boldness, Piety, Temper). They grow a year older each year — their portrait ages with them. On a scene, whoever fits the moment speaks — and they disagree. Competence decides what they can pull off; temperament decides what they urge. The second-in-command casts your workings by default.

Every Wizard practises one of the seven colour schools (Red, Yellow, Brown, Green, Blue, Purple, White) and holds one of nine classes: Magus, Sorcerer, Druid, Necromancer, Pyromancer, Enchanter, Charmer, Chaos Mage, Ghost Eater. School is the flavour of the magic; class is what they do best. Who stands in your Circle shapes what workings the coven can attempt.

Spells the coven casts on its own initiative, on the Workings screen. Each belongs to a colour school and tests one competence (Power, Wisdom, Guile, or Courage) against a difficulty. You choose the caster — the second-in-command casts by default. A caster whose school matches the working, backed by high mastery, casts at far better odds; on a win the working takes full effect, on a loss a weaker one. Nothing is ever certain.

How deeply the coven commands each of the seven schools (0–100), separate from your standing with the cults. Opening mastery reflects who sits in your Circle — the schools your Wizards practise start strong, the rest near nothing. Casting a working of a school practises it, nudging that mastery up. Higher mastery raises the odds on every working of that colour.
Wizards transformed by the Flame. When a Circle member is taken, the loss is permanent — they leave the Circle but persist in the world as a Soul you may later meet, bargain with, or mourn.

Fragments of the old world the coven recovers — a burned tome, a survivor's account, a cautionary tale of some great Wizard who reached for an epic working and failed catastrophically as the memory drained out from under them. Each fills in the Codex and, piece by piece, the truth of the Flame and the Embers. The Blue Bastion and its Forgotten Athenaeum chase them hardest.

Your coven seat, and one of the scattered hearths — a few dozen souls keeping an EMBER of the Sacred Flame alive in a guttering hearth-rune. It is the coven's heart and its last thread to the old power: let it die and Runehold dies with it. Grow it from a hearth to a power, or dwindle into a Forgotten hold.

actions.label: Drive the Harvest
actions.desc: Push the whole coven into the fields from dawn to dark. The granary fills; the spirit frays.

actions.label: Bless the Fields
actions.desc: Coax the crops with growth-runes and a little spent mana.

actions.label: Buy Seed-Grain
actions.desc: Send coin down the market roads for grain and good seed.

actions.label: Forage the Wastes
actions.desc: Send gatherers into the broken lands. There is food out there — and worse than food.

actions.label: Sell Rune-Craft
actions.desc: Send worked charms and minor enchantments down the market roads for coin.

actions.label: Buy Mana-Reagents
actions.desc: Purchase rare salts and ash-glass to replenish the coven's reserve.

actions.label: Trade the Surplus
actions.desc: Sell what the granary can spare while the price is good.

actions.label: Court the Red Factors
actions.desc: Grease the Stock Sanctuary's palms; the Gilded Reach remembers a paying friend.

actions.label: Raid the Caravans
actions.desc: Fall on the trade-roads and take what Runehold needs. The world remembers a raider.

actions.label: Muster the Coven
actions.desc: Call every hand to arms and feed them well; a coven that can fight stands taller.

actions.label: Fortify the Hold
actions.desc: Raise watchfires and deepen the walls against what the Fracture sends.

actions.label: Hire Warrior Mercenaries
actions.desc: Steel you cannot cast walks your walls — and the coven mutters at trusting those who fear the Flame.

actions.label: Mend the Wards
actions.desc: Reinforce the rune-wards of Runehold against the spreading Fracture. A White working — order against decay.

win.text: The wards flare and hold. The Fracture's creep slows against Runehold's stones.

lose.text: The runes gutter half-set. The wards barely hold, and the mana is spent all the same.

actions.label: Deepen the Runes
actions.desc: Chant the old runes deeper into memory, holding back the Forgetting. A Blue working — knowledge against loss.

win.text: The lore sinks in true. The coven's memory deepens against the dark.

lose.text: The chant slips; only fragments take. A little is remembered, most is not.

actions.label: Tend the Hearth-Flame
actions.desc: Keep the hearth-rune bright through the long dark; the coven takes heart. A Green working — nurture against despair.

win.text: The hearth-rune blooms warm and steady. The coven sleeps easier for it.

lose.text: The flame sputters low. It holds, but the warmth does not reach far.

actions.label: Court the Sacred Flame
actions.desc: Draw on the Flame itself — beyond any school. Its regard grows, but drawing deep stirs what should stay still. A test of nerve, not colour.

win.text: The Flame answers, and does not take. Its regard settles on Runehold like a warm weight.

lose.text: The Flame stirs and turns cold. It grants a little regard, but something has woken that should have slept.

coven.text: Faith is the only wall that never falls. Tend the coven's heart before its stones.
Sentiment won't fill a belly. Keep them fed and they'll keep the faith.
A coven grows by daring. Send me where the danger is — the others will follow.
Guard who we have. Every Wizard lost is a rune we never chant again.
Half these apprentices have never bled for the hold. That should change.
Their faith is guttering like the hearth-rune. Mend it now, or we lose them one by one.
The coven would follow you into the Flame itself. Don't spend that trust lightly.
We are what remains of the old Circle. Lead us well, and we hold.

fields.text: The stores won't stretch to the thaw. We plant now, or we starve in it.
Let me lead a forage into the deep wastes. I'll come back heavy, or not at all.
Bless the fields. The Flame has fed us through darker springs than this.
Buy seed-grain while the Reach still trades. Coin keeps better than hope.
Drive the harvest hard. Backs will ache, but bellies won't.
The stores are nearly bare. This is no season for half-measures — we plant or forage now.
We're a bad week from famine. Whatever you mean to do, do it this season.
A coven cannot eat runes. Mind the stores before they mind us.

market.text: Coin is the one magic the Gilded Reach respects. Spend it before it spends us.
Don't owe the Reach a favour. Their debts come due in blood.
I can turn this coin twice before the factors notice. Let me do the dealing.
Trade if we must — but do not kneel to aristocrats who forgot the Flame.
Buy Red's favour now. We'll want a friend with a full purse before long.
The purse is near empty. We've little left to trade our way out of trouble.
Give the coin a job, or it does nothing at all.

war.text: Raid the caravans. Steel fears the magic that strikes first.
Every raid stirs the Fracture. Fortify — don't provoke what we can't unmake.
Hire mercenaries and let them bleed for us. I'd sooner cast than swing a blade.
The coven distrusts steel it cannot enchant. Do not lean on hired swords.
Muster the coven. If the Warriors come, let them find us ready.
The ash is at the walls. Fortify, or the Fracture ends this hold before any Warrior does.
Magic against steel — choose the ground before they choose it for you.

workings.text: My hands are steady. Give me the hard working and stand well back.
Deepen the runes while I still remember them. The Forgetting takes the old spells first.
Court the Flame if you dare — but send someone whose soul you can spare.
Mend the wards first. A working undone is cheaper than a hold unmade.
Match the caster to the colour. Waste no mana on a spell that fights the hand that casts it.
We're all but dry. One working, chosen well — and no more this season.
Mend the wards before anything else. The Fracture is close enough to feel now.
The old runes are slipping from us. Deepen them while any of us still remember them.
Mana sits idle. Put it to work before it's needed and gone.

cults.text: Choose your friends among the seven with care. Every ally is another's enemy.
A cult wronged remembers longer than a cult befriended. Mind your feuds.
Play the colours against each other. We're too small to fight them, big enough to tip them.
The Blue Bastion still keeps the old lore. Court them before the Forgetting wins it.
Let them circle. A hold that flinches at every cult never grows.
Seven cults, and no pleasing all of them at once. Choose.

map.text: The Secret Tower waits out there in the dark. Someone must dare the road to it.
Every hold on that map can become a grave. Scout before you stride.
All roads bend back to the Tower and its Flame. That is the only road that matters.
The world forgets itself a little more each season. Map it while it still holds a shape.
Look how the ash has eaten the edges. Soon there'll be no map left to read.
The Flame leans toward us. The Tower is nearer now than it has ever been — press on.
Runehold at the centre, the ash at the edge. Watch which of them grows.

saga.text: What we do is written whether we will it or not. Give the chroniclers worthy ink.
The saga keeps no lies. Read it when you doubt the road we've walked.
Let them write that Runehold dared. Better a bright saga than a long grey one.
If this is to be our last chapter, let it read that we did not go quietly.
Every choice becomes a line in this. Make it a saga worth the telling.

creation.title: Keeper of the Sacred Flame
body: Once the Sacred Flame burned whole in the Secret Tower, and the world was whole with it. Then its keepers vanished, the Tower went dark — and the world began, slowly, to forget.

As the Tower’s enemies gathered, the coven that kept it broke the Flame into Embers and scattered them beyond reach. One was carried to Runehold, to your hearth-rune. You are its Archon now. Let the fire die, and Runehold dies with it.
Before the saga begins, a few things must be settled — who you are, and what you believe.

creation.title: Your Circle
creation.body: These few keep the Ember with you. They will counsel you and quarrel among themselves; lead them well.

creation.title: Your Colour
creation.prompt: Every Wizard leans toward one of the seven schools. Which do you name your own?

options.label: Red
options.desc: Coin, courts, and the Gilded Reach. Power bought and sold.

options.label: Yellow
options.desc: The bending of time and season. Patience made into power.

options.label: Brown
options.desc: Earth, craft, and the practical working. Magic that feeds a hold.

options.label: Green
options.desc: Growing things and the wild. Nurture against the dark.

options.label: Blue
options.desc: Lore and memory; the Bastion and its Athenaeum. Knowledge above all.

options.label: Purple
options.desc: Chaos and the unmaking of certainty. Power without a leash.

options.label: White
options.desc: Order and clarity against the Forgetting. The wall that holds.

creation.title: How You Came to Lead
creation.prompt: The seat of Archon was not empty. How did it become yours?

options.label: The dying keeper named you
options.desc: The last Archon gave you the fire with their final breath. The coven honours the choice — and watches to see if it was wise.

options.label: The coven raised you
options.desc: They chose you from among themselves. Their faith in you is real — and yours to keep or lose.

options.label: You took it by force
options.desc: The old Archon is a Forgotten Soul now, and not by the Flame’s choosing. The coven follows — warily.

creation.title: How You Came to Magic
creation.prompt: Every Wizard comes to the craft by some road. Which was yours?

options.label: Born to it
options.desc: The runes were in your blood before you could speak them. Magic is as natural to you as breath.

options.label: Studied for it
options.desc: You earned every working through years of the Athenaeum’s cold libraries. Nothing was given.

options.label: Bargained for it
options.desc: You reached the craft by a door better left shut, and paid a price you don’t speak of.

creation.title: What Magic Is
creation.prompt: The wise argue over what magic truly is. What do you believe?

options.label: Memory
options.desc: Magic is the world remembering how it once worked — and it is being forgotten.

options.label: A gift
options.desc: Magic is given, from the Flame or something older, to those who are meant to carry it.

options.label: A tool
options.desc: Magic is a power like any other, to be wielded by whoever is strong enough to hold it.

creation.title: The Warriors
creation.prompt: The Warriors outnumber the Wizards and need no spells to kill. How do you regard them?

options.label: Necessary allies
options.desc: Steel guards what magic cannot. Treat with them, and Runehold stands the stronger.

options.label: A lesser breed
options.desc: They cannot cast a single rune. Let them serve, and keep them at arm’s length.

options.label: Enemies in waiting
options.desc: They fear what they can’t do, and fear becomes the knife. Better to strike first.
